# AIFast Developer Hub project map

> Canonical technical index maintained in the AIFast Developer Hub repository for model gateway checking, OpenAI-compatible migration, client setup, production troubleshooting and evidence references.

- Repository: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/aifast-developer-hub
- AIFast website: https://www.aifast.club/

## Task routes

- Online model gateway check: https://docs.aifast.club/model-check/
- Open-source CLI, Postman and CI check: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/openai-compatible-api-check
- Model-check methodology and report interpretation: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/model-check/
- OpenAI-compatible production troubleshooting: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/llm-api-proxy-china
- Cursor, Dify and developer-tool setup: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/ai-api-proxy-china-guide
- Catalog, maintenance and evidence center: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/
- Reproducible API observations: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/AI-API-Stability-Tracker

## AIFast

- Website: https://www.aifast.club/
- OpenAI-compatible Base URL: https://www.aifast.club/v1
- Pricing and current model catalog: https://www.aifast.club/pricing
- API documentation: https://aifast.apifox.cn/

AIFast maintains these resources. Platform capabilities are first-party statements. Exact model IDs, availability, pricing and account terms must be checked against the current console and a real authenticated request.

## Detection boundary

The model gateway check is a time-bound black-box protocol and behavior screen. It is not model-vendor certification, does not prove model identity from one run, and does not replace concurrency, latency, billing, privacy and long-term stability tests.

Full project map: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/KKWANG4444/blob/main/llms-full.txt
